title: Finite GMRES Residual Shadow Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite GMRES bad one-step alpha QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact rational one-step GMRES replay computes alpha = 2/5 for a fixed 2x2 system, while a malformed row claims alpha = 1/2, isolating a compact scalar equality conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional Krylov rows only when they add distinct Arnoldi/GMRES least-squares, restart, preconditioner, breakdown, nonnormality, or numerical-honesty pressure; keep general GMRES convergence and floating-point stability in future theorem or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed matrix, right-hand side, initial residual, one-dimensional Krylov direction, exact minimizer coefficient, residual, orthogonality equation, and residual-norm decrease as deterministic rational replay obligations.
Route the false one-step GMRES coefficient claim through checked QF_LRA/Farkas evidence after replay computes the exact coefficient.
Expose the pack through residual-bound, finite-operator/Krylov, inner-product projection, matrix/numerical, and functional/operator learner and consumer query surfaces.

Keep general GMRES convergence, Arnoldi/least-squares theorem schemas, restarted GMRES, preconditioning, breakdown behavior, nonnormal spectral theory, and floating-point Krylov stability marked as future Lean or numerical-honesty work until formal routes exist.
